data.title: Safety Insurance agrees to $1.54B Mapfre buyout deal; shares soar 35% in after-hours
data.description:
• Safety Insurance Group (SAFT) announced on Thursday that it has agreed to be acquired by an affiliate of Mapfre in an all-cash deal valued at ~$1.54B.
  • The shareholders will receive $105 per share in cash, representing a 44% premium to the stock's closing price near $72.94.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2027, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • After the deal closes, Safety Insurance will continue operating under its existing brand as a wholly owned subsidiary of Mapfre U.S.A.
  • CEO George Murphy said the deal delivers an exceptional outcome for shareholders and will provide greater financial strength, technology, and growth opportunities while maintaining Safety's commitment to customers and agents.
  • On the market side, the Safety Insurance stock surged over 35% to $99.01 in the after-hours trading session.

data.title: Stripe in talks to acquire AI startup OpenRouter for ~$10B - report
data.description:
Payments firm Stripe (STRIP) is in discussions to acquire the AI startup OpenRouter for about $10B, people familiar with the matter told The Wall Street Journal.

A transaction could be announced soon, according to the sources.

Other big tech companies had also been looking to acquire OpenRouter, the report published on Thursday noted.

OpenRouter sells software that allows customers to easily access AI models built by OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) as well as free-to-download alternatives.

The firm's backers include CapitalG, the growth-stage venture capital fund of Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) (GOOG:CA).

The transaction is expected to help Stripe expand in the AI market, WSJ noted.

Recently, Stripe was also said to be planning to acquire ​PayPal (PYPL) (PYPL:CA) jointly with private equity firm Advent International in a $60.50 per share, or over $53B, deal.

However, PayPal's board reportedly sees the $53B takeover bid as undervaluing the company and facing regulatory and financing hurdles.

data.title: Ovintiv GAAP EPS of $1.62 misses by $0.49
data.description:
• Ovintiv press release (OVV): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.62 misses by $0.49.
  • Recognized a net gain on risk management in revenues of $122 million, before tax
  • Generated cash from operating activities of $1.6 billion and Non-GAAP Cash Flow of approximately $1.3 billion
  • Second quarter average total production volumes were approximately 615 MBOE/d, including 206 Mbbls/d of oil and condensate, 82 Mbbls/d of other NGLs (C2 to C4) and 1,959 MMcf/d of natural gas
  • Second quarter capital investment of $574 million was at the midpoint of the guidance range of $550 million to $600 million
  • Reported second quarter upstream operating expense of $3.25 per BOE, upstream transportation and processing costs of $9.47 per BOE, production, mineral and other taxes of $1.43 per BOE, or 3.5% of upstream product revenue
  • Excluding the impact of hedges, second quarter average realized price for oil and condensate was $97.50 per barrel (105% of WTI), $21.67 per barrel for other NGLs, and $1.71 per Mcf (59% of NYMEX) for natural gas, resulting in a total average realized price of $41.00 per BOE
  • Including the impact of hedges, second quarter average realized price for oil and condensate was $91.22 per barrel (98% of WTI), $21.67 per barrel for other NGLs, and $1.99 per Mcf (69% of NYMEX) for natural gas, resulting in a total average realized price of $39.79 per BOE

  • 2026 Guidance

    3Q 2026

    Full Year 2026

    Total Production (MBOE/d)

    615 – 640

    630 – 645

    Oil & Condensate (Mbbls/d) 

    205 – 210

    210 – 212

    NGLs (C2 to C4) (Mbbls/d)

    75 – 80

    83 – 85

    Natural Gas (MMcf/d)

    2,000 – 2,100

    2,025 – 2,075

    Capital Investment ($ Millions)

    $550 – $600

    $2,250 – $2,350

data.title: CNB Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.63, revenue of $87.6M
data.description:
• CNB Financial press release (CCNE): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.63.
  • Revenue of $87.6M.
  • As of June 30, 2026, the Corporation’s total shareholders’ equity was $909.4 million, representing an increase of $20.3 million, or 2.29%, from March 31, 2026, and an increase of $272.2 million, or 42.71%, from June 30, 2025. The quarter-over-quarter increase was primarily driven by growth in retained earnings of $21.6 million, net of the payment of common and preferred stock dividends, partially offset by a $2.3 million increase in accumulated other comprehensive loss. The year-over-year increase was primarily driven by a $206.1 million increase in additional paid-in capital related to the ESSA acquisition, growth in retained earnings of $69.9 million, net of the payment of common and preferred stock dividends, partially offset by a $3.1 million increase in accumulated other comprehensive loss, during the twelve months ended June 30, 2026.

data.title: Rheinmetall breaks ground on major German plant to expand ammunition output
data.description:
Rheinmetall (RNMBF)(RNMBY) said Thursday it begun construction of one of Europe's largest propellant powder plants, expanding ammunition production capacity in Germany as European countries accelerate defense spending.

The new facility will be built at the Aschau am Inn site in Bavaria, home to Rheinmetall subsidiary Nitrochemie Aschau. Production is scheduled to begin in 2027, with full capacity expected in 2028.

 

 

The expansion comes as European governments seek to replenish weapons stockpiles depleted by military aid to Ukraine following Russia's invasion. NATO members have also increased defense budgets after President Donald Trump renewed pressure on freeloading European allies to shoulder a larger share of the alliance's defense spending, fueling demand for ammunition and the industrial capacity needed to produce it.

Rheinmetall (RNMBF)(RNMBY) said it will invest several hundred million euros in the project. The company expects the site to expand from 90 hectares to about 110 hectares and create several hundred additional jobs, increasing total employment to about 1,400.

The Aschau facility currently produces about 1,700 metric tons of propellant powder and 300,000 propellant charge modules each year. Over the next 24 months, Rheinmetall said production will more than double, adding about 2,500 metric tons of powder, more than 5 million combustible components and more than 1 million modular propellant charge modules annually.

Propellant powder is a critical component of artillery ammunition and other large-caliber weapons. It generates the gas pressure that propels a projectile from a gun barrel.

The project forms part of Rheinmetall's Firepower initiative, which aims to expand ammunition manufacturing capacity across Europe. By 2030, the company plans to increase annual propellant powder production to 20,000 metric tons, with about 4,200 metric tons expected to come from the Aschau site.

Rheinmetall said it plans to invest about €650 million in propellant powder production and related precursor materials, including about €350 million at Aschau. The expansion also includes production facilities in Switzerland and Spain.

The company said the Aschau site will manage the full production chain for modular propellant charges, from raw materials through finished components.

data.title: SpaceX delays Starship flight test to Friday as weather hampers heat shield imaging
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) postponed the 13th flight test of its Starship rocket to Friday, July 24, after weather conditions threatened one of the mission's key objectives: capturing detailed ground-based images of the vehicle's heat shield during ascent.

The company announced the delay Thursday in a post on X, saying poor weather would prevent engineers from obtaining clear imagery of Starship's thermal protection system as the rocket passes through a period of high dynamic pressure shortly after liftoff.

"Now targeting Friday, July 24 for Starship's thirteenth flight test, due to weather," SpaceX (SPCX) said. "A key objective for the flight test is to get clear imagery from the ground of Starship's heatshield as it flies at a higher dynamic pressure during ascent, which won't be possible with today's."

For investors, Starship is more than an experimental rocket. It sits at the center of SpaceX's (SPCX) long-term strategy to dramatically increase launch capacity while lowering the cost of reaching orbit. The company has invested more than $15 billion in developing the vehicle and expanding infrastructure at its Starbase launch site, describing Starship as critical to deploying larger Starlink satellite constellations, expanding satellite-to-mobile services and eventually supporting orbital AI computing infrastructure.

First launched in 2023, Starship is designed to become the world's first fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle. SpaceX (SPCX) says the next-generation Starship V3 is intended to carry up to 100 metric tons into Earth orbit while enabling aircraft-like turnaround times through rapid refurbishment and reuse. Future versions are expected to double that payload capacity.

The company has already demonstrated the ability to recover and catch Starship's Super Heavy booster using giant mechanical arms, known as "chopsticks," on the launch tower. SpaceX (SPCX) believes that capability will eventually support multiple launches per day while reducing launch costs by as much as 99% compared with historical industry averages.

Starship is also expected to begin delivering commercial payloads to orbit during the second half of 2026, making progress on the test program increasingly important to the company's broader commercial ambitions.

At the same time, SpaceX (SPCX) has cautioned investors that unexpected design changes, supply chain disruptions, technical anomalies, environmental issues and other engineering challenges could delay Starship's development and, in turn, slow the deployment of next-generation satellites and other space-based services.

Thursday's postponement illustrates the emphasis SpaceX (SPCX) places on collecting engineering data during test flights. While weather posed no fundamental obstacle to launching the vehicle, it would have prevented the company from obtaining the high-quality imagery needed to evaluate how Starship's heat shield performs under the intense aerodynamic forces experienced during ascent.

data.title: Primis Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.23 misses by $0.15, revenue of $55.7M beats by $21.34M
data.description:
